scope: S3 scope and remediation planning readiness gate for IF09-FIND-001.
s3_scope_statement: The scope remains limited to the verified medium-severity active_context_governance finding IF09-FIND-001 from the IF10 handoff graph.
The S3 focus is hardening-oriented and bounded to canonical source packet SHA verification drift handling, not to runtime mutation, product behavior, or external-system execution.
This gate may define readiness, evidence, and blocking criteria only; it may not reproduce the failure, open RED baseline execution, compile a remediation plan, apply a fix, or close the finding.
future_purg02_readiness_criteria: The IF10 graph, handoff manifest, and source packet hashes must remain stable and traceable to IF09-FIND-001.
The reproduction, replay, and mutation unit references for IF09-FIND-001 must remain explicit and unambiguous before any RED baseline opening decision.
A future PURG-02 opener must confirm that the intended reproduction activity does not silently expand into remediation, runtime real, or real_apply scope.
A future PURG-02 opener must preserve the roadmap-defined boundary that PURG-02 reproduces before any fix and that PURG-03 remains the separate remediation planning phase.
Any future PURG-02 decision must remain operator-mediated and cannot be auto-opened from this readiness artifact.
future_purg02_required_evidence: Stable hash references for the IF10 handoff graph and handoff manifest.
Stable hash reference for the operator source packet used in PURG-01 gates.
Explicit reproduction_unit, replay_unit, and mutation_unit references bound to IF09-FIND-001.
A written statement that the RED baseline objective is to prove the finding before any fix and that no remediation action is included in scope.
A no-real-execution and no-real-apply attestation carried forward into any future PURG-02 opening decision.
future_purg02_blocked_conditions: Evidence-chain drift or hash mismatch across the IF10 graph, handoff manifest, or operator source packet.
Ambiguous or missing reproduction, replay, or mutation unit references for IF09-FIND-001.
Any attempt to combine PURG-02 reproduction with PURG-03 remediation planning or with fix application in the same gate.
Any requirement to invert runtime, remediation, finding-close, product, Bedrock, secrets, or real_apply locks.
Any proposal that treats this readiness artifact as automatic authorization to open PURG-02.
